CREATE TABLE test_array (id UInt32, arr Array(Int32));
SELECT arrayPartialShuffle(arr, 10) FROM test_array WHERE id = 1;
SELECT arrayPartialShuffle(arr, 5) FROM (SELECT arr FROM test_array WHERE id = 2);
CREATE TABLE test_metrics (metric_name String, values Array(Int32));
SELECT metric_name, arrayPartialShuffle(values, 4) as shuffled_values FROM (SELECT * FROM test_metrics) WHERE metric_name = 'metric_b';
SELECT arrayPartialShuffle([100, 200, 300], 2) AS result UNION ALL SELECT arrayPartialShuffle([400, 500, 600], 1);
SELECT id, arrayPartialShuffle(arr, 8) FROM test_array WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_array WHERE id > 0);
CREATE TABLE random_data AS SELECT arrayPartialShuffle(arr, 2) AS shuffled_arr FROM (SELECT [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] AS arr);
SELECT arrayPartialShuffle(arr, 0) FROM test_array HAVING id = 1;
SELECT arrayPartialShuffle(arr, 5) FROM test_array WHERE arr[1] = 1;
SELECT arrayPartialShuffle(arr, 5) FROM (SELECT arr FROM test_array WHERE id = 1);
EXPECTED_RESULTS AS (SELECT arrayPartialShuffle(arr, 3) AS expected FROM test_array); SELECT expected FROM EXPECTED_RESULTS;
